description: Security Analytics Platform - Index workflow output data into OpenSearch for dashboards, queries, and alerts

userStories.title: Add OpenSearch client dependency and configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the OpenSearch client library installed and configured so that the backend can communicate with OpenSearch.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @opensearch-project/opensearch package to backend dependencies
Create OpenSearch client configuration in backend config module
Environment variables: OPENSEARCH_URL, OPENSEARCH_USERNAME, OPENSEARCH_PASSWORD
Client exported from a dedicated module (e.g., opensearch.client.ts)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: OpenSearchClient created in config/opensearch.client.ts, config in config/opensearch.config.ts, exported from AppModule

userStories.title: Create Analytics service for document indexing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Analytics service that can index documents to OpenSearch with proper metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Create AnalyticsService in backend (analytics.service.ts)
Method: indexDocument(orgId, document, options) that indexes to security-findings-{orgId}-{YYYY.MM.DD}
Method: bulkIndex(orgId, documents, options) for array outputs
Standard metadata added: workflow_id, workflow_name, run_id, node_ref, component_id, @timestamp
Auto-detect asset_key from fields: host, domain, subdomain, url, ip, asset, target
Service registered in analytics module
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: SecurityAnalyticsService created with indexDocument and bulkIndex methods, registered in AnalyticsModule

userStories.title: Create index template for security-findings indices
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an index template setup script that creates proper mappings for analytics data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create setup script or migration for index template: security-findings-*

Template includes mappings: @timestamp (date), workflow_id (keyword), workflow_name (keyword), run_id (keyword), node_ref (keyword), component_id (keyword), asset_key (keyword), data (object with dynamic: true)
Template settings: number_of_shards: 1, number_of_replicas: 1
Script can be run via npm command (e.g., npm run setup:opensearch)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Created backend/scripts/setup-opensearch.ts with index template setup, added npm script setup:opensearch

userStories.title: Define Analytics Sink component schema
userStories.description: As a workflow builder, I need the Analytics Sink component defined so it appears in the component registry.
acceptanceCriteria: Add analytics-sink component definition to component registry
Component category: 'Outputs'
Component accepts any input type
Parameters: indexSuffix (optional string), assetKeyField (optional string), failOnError (optional boolean, default false)
Component has appropriate icon and description
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Created analytics-sink component in worker/src/components/core/analytics-sink.ts, registered in index.ts, category: output, icon: BarChart3

userStories.title: Implement Analytics Sink execution logic in worker
userStories.description: As a workflow executor, I need the Analytics Sink to actually index data when the workflow runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add analytics-sink handler in worker execution code
Handler receives input from upstream node
If input is array, call bulkIndex with each item as separate document
If input is object, call indexDocument with single document
Include workflow context: workflow_id, workflow_name, run_id, node_ref
Use component_id from upstream node that produced the data
Indexing is fire-and-forget by default (does not fail workflow)
If failOnError is true, throw on indexing failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Implemented execute() logic in analytics-sink component, created OpenSearchIndexer utility, extended ExecutionContext with workflow metadata

userStories.title: Add retry logic and error handling to Analytics service
userStories.description: As a workflow builder, I need analytics indexing to be resilient to transient failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s delays)
Log indexing errors to workflow trace via trace service
Error details include: index name, document count, error message
Successful indexing logged as trace event
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Added retryWithBackoff helper function, trace logging for success/error/partial-failure cases in both indexDocument and bulkIndex methods

userStories.title: Add Analytics Sink to workflow editor palette
userStories.description: As a workflow builder, I want to see Analytics Sink in the component palette so I can add it to workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics Sink appears in 'Outputs' category in component palette
Component has icon consistent with design system (chart/analytics icon)
Component can be dragged onto canvas
Component can receive connections from any other node
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed: Analytics Sink appears in Output category, uses BarChart3 icon, is draggable/placeable, verified in browser. Fixed OpenSearchClient DI by creating OpenSearchModule.

userStories.title: Create Analytics Sink configuration panel
userStories.description: As a workflow builder, I want to configure the Analytics Sink parameters in the workflow editor.
acceptanceCriteria: When Analytics Sink node is selected, configuration panel shows
Panel has text input for 'Index Suffix' with placeholder showing default (workflow slug)
Panel has dropdown for 'Asset Key Field' with options: Auto-detect, host, domain, subdomain, url, ip, asset, target, or custom
Panel has checkbox for 'Fail workflow if indexing fails'
Configuration saves to node parameters
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed: Enhanced component parameters with dropdown for assetKeyField, placeholder for indexSuffix, conditional customAssetKeyField parameter. ConfigPanel auto-discovers and renders parameters.

userStories.title: Add Dashboards link to Studio sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to access analytics dashboards from the Studio sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Dashboards' item added to sidebar navigation after 'Artifact Library'
Icon: BarChart3 or similar analytics icon from lucide-react
Clicking opens external URL in new tab
URL constructed as: OPENSEARCH_DASHBOARDS_URL (from env) or configurable
Link includes org context if needed for tenant routing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed: Added Dashboards navigation item with BarChart3 icon, opens in new tab, conditional on VITE_OPENSEARCH_DASHBOARDS_URL env var. Note: org context routing can be added later if needed.

userStories.title: Create Analytics query API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to query analytics data via API for integrations and custom visualizations.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/analytics/query endpoint created
Request body accepts: query (OpenSearch DSL), size (number), from (number), aggs (object)
Endpoint automatically scopes query to user's org index pattern: security-findings-{orgId}-*
Returns OpenSearch response (hits, aggregations, total)
Authentication required (Clerk session)
Returns 400 for invalid query syntax
Endpoint documented in OpenAPI spec or API docs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Created AnalyticsController with POST /api/analytics/query endpoint, added query() method to SecurityAnalyticsService, supports OpenSearch DSL queries with org scoping

userStories.title: Add rate limiting to Analytics API
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need rate limiting on the analytics API to prevent abuse.
acceptanceCriteria: Rate limit applied to POST /api/analytics/query: 100 requests per minute per user
Returns 429 Too Many Requests when limit exceeded
Rate limit headers included in response: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining
Rate limiting uses existing rate limit infrastructure or Redis
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Installed @nestjs/throttler, configured ThrottlerModule with Redis storage, applied rate limiting to analytics query endpoint with headers documentation

userStories.title: Create Analytics settings page for retention configuration
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to configure data retention settings for my organization.
acceptanceCriteria: New settings page or section: Analytics Settings
Displays current retention period (read from org settings)
Shows retention limit based on subscription tier (Free: 30d, Pro: 90d, Enterprise: 365d)
Dropdown to select retention period within tier limit
Save button updates org analytics settings
Shows current storage usage if available (or placeholder)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed: Created AnalyticsSettingsPage with tier-based retention UI, routing, navigation. Ready for API integration in US-013.

userStories.title: Create retention settings API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints to get and update analytics retention settings.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/analytics/settings returns current retention config for org
PUT /api/analytics/settings updates retention config
Validates retention period is within tier limits
Returns 400 if retention exceeds tier limit
Only org admins can update settings (authorization check)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Created organization_settings table, OrganizationSettingsService, analytics settings DTOs, GET and PUT endpoints with tier-based validation and admin-only authorization

userStories.title: Add View Analytics button to workflow detail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly view analytics for a specific workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'View Analytics' button on workflow detail/edit page header
Button opens OpenSearch Dashboards in new tab
URL includes pre-filtered query parameter: workflow_id:{workflowId}
Button uses external link icon to indicate opens in new tab
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed: Added View Analytics button to TopBar component, conditionally shown when VITE_OPENSEARCH_DASHBOARDS_URL is set, opens in new tab with workflow_id filter

userStories.title: Add Analytics Sink to docker-compose for development
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OpenSearch running locally for development and testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add opensearch service to docker-compose.yml (single-node, dev config)
Add opensearch-dashboards service to docker-compose.yml
Services use environment variables for configuration
Health check configured for opensearch service
README updated with instructions to start analytics stack
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed: Added OpenSearch and OpenSearch Dashboards services to docker-compose.infra.yml, updated installation.mdx with Analytics Stack section, updated .env.example files
